english: hole, sgoi (sgo'i) key-hole Dzl.
name of a learned Lama and author of čos-byuṅ (chos byung), about the year 1300 Glr., an adherent of the Adi-buddha doctrine, v. Cs. Gram.
Sch., idle talk, tittle-tattle.
snow-storm Mil.

Hindi गुड़, guṛ, hence W. *gu-rám* (*gu ram*), raw sugar, muscovado; treacle, Mil., Lt.; bu-rám (bu ram) sgór-ba (sgor ba) to boil down raw sugar Lex.; bu-ram-šiṅ (bu ram shing), bur-šiṅ (bur shing), vulg. *gur-šiṅ* (*gur shing*) sugar-cane; bu-ram-šiṅ-pa (bu ram shing pa), इक्ष्वाकु, name of the first king of the solar dynasty in India, Glr.; bu-ram-čaṅ (bu ram chang), bur-čaṅ (bur chang) sugar-beer Lt.; bur-dkár (bur dkar) Lt. bur-stáṅ (bur stang) yčig (gcig) (more correctly ltaṅ (ltang)) Sch., a bale of raw sugar packed up in leather.

(cf. bun (bun)) advanced money, debt, *ṅul (*ngul) gye (gye) bú-lon (bu lon) mi - la (mi la) táṅ-čè* (*tang che*) W. to lend a person a hundred florins; (bú-lon (bu lon) byéd-pa (byed pa) to contract debts Schr., Sch.??) bú-lon (bu lon) ǰál-ba ('jal ba) (W. *čàl-če* (*cal che*)), spród-pa (sprod pa) Sch. to pay a debt, sél-ba (sel ba) to put out, to cancel a debt, déd-pa ('ded pa), bdá-ba (bda' ba) to call in, to recover a debt, čágs-pa (chags pa) prob. the beginning and running up of debts Dzl.; bú-lon-pa (bu lon pa) debtor, dṅul (dngul) brgyai (brgya'i) of a hundred rupees.
v. sbugs-hág (sbugs hag).
1. sbst. hole, búg-pa (bug pa) ˳búg-pa ('bug pa) to bore holes Glr., cog. to bú-ga (bu ga). — 2. Sch., to get holes (?).
v. sbugs (sbugs).
Ld. birdsfoot-trefoil, Melilotus.

1. a humming and stinging insect, bee etc.; buṅ-lčág (bung lcag) sting, and also the wound caused by it; *buṅ-ba (*bung ba) čàg (cag) taṅ (tang) soṅ* (song*) W. the bee has stung. 2. Cs. a bright black stone.

mass, heap, bulk, buṅs-čén (bungs chen) a large heap Lt.; dri-čúi (dri chu'i) buṅs-čé (bungs che) a great quantity of urine Miṅ. (Ming.); buṅs (bungs) byéd-pa (byed pa) to heap one upon another, pile up. — buṅ (bung) many (?).

every darkening of the air through dry matter, a cloud of dust, more exactly tsal-búd (tshal bud); bud-tsúb (bud tshub) dust from threshing; búd-kyis (bud kyis) btab (btab) wrapt in vapour Mil.; perh. also snow-storm (Sch.), yet not exclusively.

Ssk., Buddha, n. of the founder of the religion which is called after him, occurring but rarely in Tibetan writings, and among the people (at least in W.) almost unknown, v. saṅs-rgyas (sangs rgyas); bud-dhai (bud dha'i) preṅ-ba (phreng ba), *búd-dé (*bud de) ṭáṅ-na* (*trang na*) rosary Ts.
1. Sch. = sbúd-pa (sbud pa). — 2. pf. cf. ˳búd-pa ('bud pa).

B. and C., 1. woman, bud-méd (bud med) sdúg-gu (sdug gu) a fair woman Dzl.; bud-méd (bud med) daṅ (dang) sbágs-pa (sbags pa) to defile, corrupt one's self with women Dzl. — 2. wife, spouse, not frq. Dzl. (W. bú-mo (bu mo) and ắ-ne (a ne)).
fire-wood, fuel, also dung used as such; búd-šiṅ (bud shing) bśág-pa (bshag pa) to cleave or chop wood.

1. = bú-lon (bu lon) Mil., bun (bun) toṅ (tong) lend us! Mil.; bun (bun) btáṅ-du (btang du) ma (ma) nyán-pas (nyan pas) not willing to lend any thing Mil.; kyéd-rnams-la (khyed rnams la) bun (bun) dgós-na (dgos na) if you want an advance (of money) Mil. — bún-to (bun tho), bún-yig (bun yig) 1. debtor's account-book. 2. bond or obligation, bill of debt. — bun-bdág (bun bdag) 1. creditor. 2. money-changer, banker. — bún-yig (bun yig) v. bún-to (bun tho). — 2. interest, *bhuṇ (*bhun) kyé'-pa* (*kye pa*) to bear interest C.; bun (bun) ǰál-ba ('jal ba) to pay interest Cs. — 3. (house) rent Sch.(?) — 4. bun-ré (bun re) Sch. a small matter, cf. bun-bún (bun bun); bun-bún (bun bun) Sch. piece-meal, scattered, dispersed. — 5. v. ˳bún-pa ('bun pa).

— ču (chu) bun-lóṅ-lóṅ (bun long long) byed (byed) it is whirling up and down, an expression used of boiling water which contains impurities or extraneous matter; hence bun-lóṅ-gi (bun long gi) snaṅ-śás (snang shas) troubled, impure, sinful thoughts.
v. ˳bub-pa ('bub pa).

= yug (yug), also taṅ (tang) (थान Hind.) 1. an entire piece of cloth rolled up; gos-bubs (gos bubs) cotton-cloth Cs. — 2. in a general sense one whole, something entire Sch.; bubs-ril (bubs ril) prob. whole, entire, bubs-ril (bubs ril) lus (lus) S.g. the whole body, opp. to separate parts.
